title: Lesson 25
paragraph: A radio report said the city will start a new program for young workers. The research shows that a clear policy can improve job performance and support a better product. A manager asked one question about property rules, while another speaker discussed race and fairness. The organization will share the report with every office.

words.meaning: work that someone does to earn money
words.example: She found a summer job at a hotel.

words.meaning: a person who controls a team, shop, or business
words.example: The manager answered the customer's question.

words.meaning: a room or building where people work at desks
words.example: The office opens at nine o'clock.

words.meaning: how well someone does a job or activity
words.example: Her performance improved after more practice.

words.meaning: a rule or plan used by a group or organization
words.example: The school has a new policy about phones.

words.meaning: something that is made or sold
words.example: This product is popular with young people.

words.meaning: a planned set of activities or a computer application
words.example: The library has a reading program for children.

words.meaning: something that a person or group owns
words.example: The sign says this land is private property.

words.meaning: a sentence or problem that asks for an answer
words.example: I have one question about the homework.

words.meaning: a competition to see who is fastest or a large human group
words.example: The race around the park starts at noon.
